attacking_style: Explosive late accelerations on punchy finishes, particularly effective when holding power 50-150m from summit. Prefers tactical patience over early moves, waiting for rivals to commit first before launching decisive counter-punch.
team_tactics: UAE appears to provide limited domestique support in key moments, forcing Hirschi into opportunistic collaborations or solo efforts. Team lacks follow-through strength when marking opponents, leaving him exposed in critical racing.
terrain_preference: Short, steep punchy climbs (Mur de Huy-style walls) where explosive acceleration trumps sustained power. Struggles in physical bar-room sprint finishes and extended cat-and-mouse scenarios requiring repeated accelerations.
vulnerability: Lacks final kick against pure puncheurs like Alaphilippe in prolonged tactical battles. Positioning errors and mechanical/interference issues cost results. Insufficient team support when marked by stronger squads exposes weaknesses in sustained efforts.
key_patterns: Wins through tactical patience, launching 50-150m from finish after opponents commit early (2 wins).
Struggles in physical sprint finishes even on punchy terrain, losing podiums to stronger finishers (2 instances).
Collaborates opportunistically with rivals when team support lacking, gambling on late attacks (2 races).
Positioning errors and interference cost potential victories despite having winning legs (2 Monument near-misses).
Shows tactical maturity beyond experience level, reading race dynamics to conserve energy for final punch.
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 Amstel showed insufficient strength when marked after strong 2020 Monument campaign. Lost explosiveness in finishing situations, suggesting declining trajectory from breakthrough 2020 season.
